section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pain to palpation of the medial aspect of the right knee with some edema and ecchymosis.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is phrased as the patient's reported experience of pain with palpation rather than an examiner-documented physical exam finding; placing it under Findings could mislead a reader into assuming it was objectively elicited on exam.

section_miscategorizations.text: He reports playing basketball only two years ago.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This historical detail is loosely related and somewhat confusing in the HPI but not clearly miscategorized; it does not fit cleanly into any other defined section, so this is a minor organizational issue rather than a true misplacement.

section_miscategorizations.text: He was unhappy about the last game.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This psychosocial detail is of questionable relevance to the presenting complaint but is still acceptable in HPI; it represents clutter more than miscategorization.
This is a valid physical exam finding but is unrelated to the knee complaint; its inclusion here is organizationally loose but not a true section error.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's heart rate is 72 bpm with a grade 2 systolic ejection murmur.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an appropriate exam finding but not clearly tied to the visit's chief complaint; it is extraneous rather than miscategorized.
